title: MIDDAG Navigation Contract
description: TypeScript contract for the sidebar navigation tree. Serialized from PHP navigation_registry during boot(). Consumed by ProductShell's SidebarNav component via usePage().props.navigation.

NavigationEntityType.description: Entity types for automatic icon resolution. When a NavigationNode has entityType set but no explicit icon, the Icon Registry resolves the icon from registerEntityIcon() mappings.
NavigationEntityType.x-ai-purpose: Use entityType instead of icon when the node represents a known entity. The icon registry will automatically resolve the correct icon, ensuring visual consistency across the app.

NavigationNode.description: A single node in the navigation tree. Can be a leaf (link) or a branch (group with children).

icon.description: Lucide icon name (kebab-case, e.g. 'graduation-cap'). Optional if entityType is set.

entityType.description: Entity type for automatic icon resolution via Icon Registry.

href.description: Navigation URL. Omit for non-navigable group headers.

badgeVariant.description: 'count' shows muted text (default). 'alert' shows a red pill badge.

active.description: Whether this node is the current page.

drilldown.description: When true, clicking replaces sidebar with this node's children + back button.

collapsible.description: When true, group renders with expand/collapse toggle.

defaultOpen.description: Whether a collapsible group starts expanded (default: false).

statusColor.description: CSS color for status dot next to drilldown title (e.g. 'var(--success)').

target.description: Link target attribute. External URLs (http/https) auto-detect '_blank'.

NavigationTreePayload.description: The complete navigation payload sent via Inertia shared props. Contains the tree, active node key, drilldown state, and persistent footer items.

activeKey.description: Key of the currently active navigation node.

drilldownStack.description: Stack of drilled-down node keys for back navigation.

footer.description: Persistent footer items (Settings, Help) always visible at sidebar bottom.

x-ai-purpose: These types describe the sidebar navigation structure. The tree is built server-side (PHP navigation_registry) and sent to the frontend via Inertia shared props. AI agents should understand this tree structure when generating pages that include navigation context. The Icon Registry (registerEntityIcon) auto-resolves icons for known entity types.
